frameworks.name: evidence accumulation / drift-diffusion
frameworks.one_line: decisions modelled as noisy accumulation of evidence with a bias term

frameworks.name: phasic arousal / pupil-linked neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: arousal transients gate the accumulation bias during choice

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: choice bias separated from sensitivity in detection tasks

core_question: Does phasic arousal suppress decision biases generally across species, modalities, and decision domains?
key_assumptions: pupil dilation indexes phasic arousal-system activity
choice biases can be decomposed into accumulation biases via DDM-like fits
biases generalize across perceptual and memory-based decisions
manipulations: spontaneous vs experimentally induced choice biases
key_findings: high arousal reduces impact of both conservative and liberal biases
bias suppression occurs in sound-detection across humans and mice
similar suppression seen when evidence is retrieved from memory
effects captured by reduced evidence-accumulation biases
interpretations: phasic arousal implements a general principle suppressing accumulation bias during decisions
